name: Jaraba-Calmarza Loop
name_original: Jaraba y Calmarza — circular desde Cetina
description: The Jaraba-Calmarza Loop, listed as a racebike route, is primarily a hiking and mountain biking trail with challenging, unpaved sections.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cetina navigate a diverse landscape characterized by the dramatic Cetina River canyon, surrounding mountainous terrain, and distinct karst formations. The region offers routes that wind alongside the river's clear waters and through historical rural areas. Cyclists encounter varied topography, including challenging ascents and scenic stretches, with roads often carved into the base of canyons. Best road cycling routes around Cetina The most popular road cycling route is Jaraba-Calmarza Loop, a 37.6 miles (60.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete, featuring moderate climbs and scenic views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ariza and Embid Castles Loop, a difficult 47.7 miles (76.8 km) path, known for its sustained ascents and panoramic vistas over the rural terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Calatayud – ️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Alhama de Aragón, a 52.8 miles (85.1 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 39 minutes. Road cycling around Cetina is defined by its river canyon, karst landscapes, and hilly sections, offering options for various ability levels from moderate to difficult. The routes in Cetina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 120 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cetina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cetina?
A: There are 14 road cycling routes around Cetina, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Cetina?
A: Road cycling around Cetina offers a mix of moderate and difficult routes. There are 5 moderate routes and 9 difficult routes, characterized by varied topography including challenging ascents and scenic stretches, often carved into the base of canyons.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, Cetina offers several challenging routes with substantial elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo Redondo Waterfall – Santuario de Jaraba loop from Alhama de Aragón&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 80.4 km route with over 1200 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992560985/ariza-and-embid-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ariza and Embid Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 76.8 km with 944 meters of climbing.

Q: What are some of the longest road cycling routes in the Cetina region?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40338307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;⭐️ Puerta de Terrer – Calatayud loop from Bubierca&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longest, covering 97.7 km. Another extensive option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31502196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calatayud – ⭐️ Puerta de Terrer loop from Alhama de Aragón&lt;/a&gt;, which is 85.1 km long.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the road cycling routes in Cetina?
A: The Cetina region is rich in natural beauty. Along your rides, you can encounter stunning karst landscapes, the dramatic Cetina River canyon, and various waterfalls. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334268" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Caprichosa Waterfall of Ibdes&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2483262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar de Mesa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5362060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges of the Mesa River&lt;/a&gt;. The famous 'Eye of the Earth' (Cetina Spring) is also a significant natural wonder in the area.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several historical and cultural points of interest. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Jaraba&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4654306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Roca Benedí&lt;/a&gt;. The town of Omiš, at the mouth of the Cetina River, is known for its pirate heritage and medieval fortresses like Fortica and Starigrad, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Cetina?
A: Many of the routes in Cetina are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992996662/jaraba-calmarza-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaraba-Calmarza Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 60.5 km route with moderate climbs and scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Cetina?
A: The Cetina region is generally pleasant for road cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, especially in the canyon, while winters can bring cooler weather. Checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always recommended.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Cetina's road cycling routes?
A: You can expect a diverse terrain featuring asphalted roads that wind through the stunning Cetina River canyon, alongside clear waters, and through historical rural landscapes. Routes often include hilly sections, hairpin bends, and roads carved into the base of canyons, providing breathtaking views of the surrounding natural beauty and karst landscapes.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or other water activities near the cycling routes?
A: The Cetina River is an adventure hub. While cycling, you'll be close to spots offering rafting, canyoning, kayaking, and stand-up paddleboarding. Radmanove Mlinice, upstream from Omiš, is a scenic area along the river that provides tranquil spots, and the Gubavica Waterfall is a significant natural wonder along the river.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Cetina?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 3 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning natural beauty of the Cetina River canyon, the diverse and challenging terrain, and the panoramic views offered by routes that wind through mountains and historical landscapes.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the road cycling trailheads in Cetina?
A: While specific public transport options directly to every trailhead may vary, the town of Omiš, located at the mouth of the Cetina River, serves as a central hub and is accessible by public transport. From Omiš, you may need to arrange local transfers or cycle to reach specific starting points for routes further into the canyon or rural areas.
tips: At this point we can take the detour to approach the Sanctuary of Jaraba, an imposing and impressive hermitage located in the Barranco de la Hoz Seca, with rocky walls next to the river canyon. A highly recommended visit if we are in the area. The guided tour is recommended.
An essential corner for nature lovers. The "Pozo Redondo" Waterfall of Calmarza is a charming place that is hidden in the lower part of the town where the Mesa River passes
We are next to the castle of Calmarza, where we can see a stately tower whose origin is unknown. It is 12 meters tall.

faqs.question: Is the Jaraba-Calmarza Loop suitable for road cycling?
faqs.answer: Despite some listings, the Jaraba-Calmarza Loop is generally not suitable for road cycling. The route includes a significant mix of unpaved, stony, and technical sections, particularly through canyons like Barranco de la Hoz Seca and Cañón del río Mesa. Some parts even feature steps equipped with staples, requiring bikes to be carried. It is primarily designed for hiking or mountain biking.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Jaraba-Calmarza Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and challenging. You'll encounter 'senderos' (paths/trails) and 'pedregoso' (stony or rocky) sections, especially within the dramatic limestone canyons. While some parts might use low-traffic roads, these are interspersed with unpaved and technical stretches, making it unsuitable for standard road bikes.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers stunning natural beauty, including dramatic limestone canyons like the Barranco de la Hoz Seca and the Cañón del río Mesa. You can also discover rock paintings and waterfalls, such as the Pozo Redondo Waterfall. The Santuario de Jaraba, nestled under rock formations, is another notable highlight, as is the Calmarza Tower.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Jaraba-Calmarza area?
faqs.answer: The region's climate is generally favorable for outdoor activities, but specific recommendations depend on your activity. For hiking or mountain biking, spring and autumn typically offer pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, especially in the canyons, while winters might bring colder conditions. Always check the local weather forecast before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Jaraba-Calmarza Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information specific to dog-friendliness on this exact loop is limited. However, as a general rule for rural trails in Spain, dogs are often permitted but should be kept on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Always practice responsible pet ownership and carry waste bags.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for the Jaraba-Calmarza Loop or the Hoces del Mesa area?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Jaraba-Calmarza Loop itself or the general Hoces del Mesa area for hiking or cycling. However, always respect private property and any local regulations or signage you may encounter.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Jaraba-Calmarza Loop?
faqs.answer: The loop can be accessed from Jaraba or Calmarza. If starting from Jaraba, parking is typically available near the Santuario de Jaraba or within the village. For Calmarza, parking options would be in or near the village center. Always check for designated parking areas upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails. Notably, it is part of the GR 24 and also crosses sections of the Camino del Cid (Senderista), GR 160 - Camino del Cid BTT, Tierras de Frontera, and Hoces del Río Mesa, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options nearby?
faqs.answer: Both Jaraba and Calmarza are small villages that offer some local amenities. Jaraba is known for its thermal baths and typically has a selection of hotels, guesthouses, and restaurants. Calmarza may have more limited options, but you can usually find local eateries or cafes. It's advisable to check availability in advance, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: What are the main challenges or considerations for this loop?
faqs.answer: The primary challenge is the varied and often difficult terrain, which includes rocky paths and technical sections unsuitable for road bikes. Additionally, a recent landslide between Jaraba and Calmarza has impacted the road, which could affect accessibility even for vehicles. Always check local conditions before attempting the route.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Jaraba-Calmarza Loop?
faqs.answer: For cycling, the estimated duration is around 2 hours and 55 minutes for the full 60.5 km loop. However, given the challenging terrain and unsuitability for road bikes, actual times for mountain biking or hiking will vary significantly based on fitness and conditions. Hikers tackling similar routes in the area report 'Hard hike' ratings requiring 'Very good fitness'.
